query.help: Search for items on Onspace. Example: onspace search query "my search term" --limit 20 --table onspace search query "keyword" --filter "price:lt:100" onspace search query "keyword" --properties "title,price"
query.usage_instructions: Search for items on Onspace. Required arguments: query. Use --table/-t; Use --limit/-l; default is 100; Use --filter/-f; Use --properties/-p.

item.help: Get details for a specific item. Example: onspace search item abc123 --table onspace search item "https://example.com/item/abc123" onspace search item abc123 --properties "title,price"
item.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific item. Required arguments: item_id. Use --table/-t; Use --properties/-p.

list.help: List items from Onspace. Example: onspace search list --limit 100 --table onspace search list --filter "status:active" onspace search list --properties "id,name,status"
list.usage_instructions: List items from Onspace. Use --table/-t; Use --limit/-l; default is 100; Use --filter/-f; Use --properties/-p.

wildcard.help: Search items with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Browser CLIs use client-side search.
wildcard.usage_instructions: Search items with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Browser CLIs use client-side search. Required arguments: query. Use --table/-t; Use --limit/-l; default is 100; Use --properties/-p.

search.usage_instructions: Use `onspace search` for search onspace. Check the leaf command flags here before running onspace search actions.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent. Use --profile/-p; Use --force/-F.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions. Use --profile/-p.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check authentication status across profiles. Use --table/-t; Use --profile/-p.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Use --table/-t; Use --profile/-p; Use --verbose/-v.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all profiles and show which is the default. Use --table/-t; Use --limit/-l; Use --filter/-f; Use --properties/-p.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific profile. Required arguments: name. Use --table/-t.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile from .env.example template. Required arguments: name.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1). Required arguments: name.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and its data. Required arguments: name. Use --force/-F.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `onspace auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `onspace auth` for manage onspace authentication. Check the leaf command flags here before running onspace auth actions.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `onspace cache` for manage response cache. Check the leaf command flags here before running onspace cache actions.

usage_instructions: Use `onspace` for cli interface for onspace (browser automation). Commands follow `onspace <group> <action>` and available groups are search, auth, cache. Use `auth` for login, status, test, and profile management; use `cache` when you need to inspect or clear cached responses.
